title: Multiple failed authentication attempts from same IPs — possible password spray / brute force
description: There are clusters of repeated authentication failures (including 'Strong Authentication is required' and 'Invalid username or password' / 'Invalid password') from the same source IPs against multiple accounts over short windows — consistent with automated credential guessing or password spray attempts. Some attempts eventually result in successful sign-ins from the same IPs or nearby addresses.

mitre.description: Brute-force / password spraying attempts against Azure AD accounts to gain valid credentials.

indicators_of_compromise: Indicators in ResultDescription: 'Invalid username or password', 'Invalid password, entered expired password', 'Strong Authentication is required.'
notes: Many 'Strong Authentication is required' failures may be conditional access / MFA prompts; however presence of 'Invalid password' / repeated failures from single IPs supports credential-guessing hypothesis. Correlate with Conditional Access and Azure AD Identity Protection telemetry (risk events) and source ASN/abuse reports for the IPs.

title: Rapid failure→success sequence for account 8f6f15e1... from same IP — suspected credential abuse or session bypass
description: Account 8f6f15e1... shows a sequence of failures immediately followed by a SUCCESS from the same IP (136.226.2.168) within minutes. Failure messages include 'Keep me signed in' interrupt and 'Strong Authentication is required', then a success — could indicate successful credential guessing, session replay, or an interactive user who resolved MFA. Requires investigation.

mitre.description: Use of valid credentials (possibly obtained via guessing or compromise) to sign in.

2025-09-04 00:54:42.218113+00:00,8f6f15e11dfc9cf773de13dc3d2fb09733513f5fa1fdd0a18eefff2300ac3fa4@lognpacific.com,Sign-in activity,SignInLogs,SUCCESS,,Azure Portal,170.85.132.167
indicators_of_compromise: ResultDescriptions showing both repeated failures and eventual success
notes: Confirm whether these successes correspond to legitimate user activity (contact user, check MFA prompts). If not, consider forcing a password reset and revoking active sessions for the account. Investigate source IP ownership and check for known VPN / proxy usage.

title: Impossible travel — same account (fa26fa6581a7be44...) signs in from Virginia (US) then Perth (AU) within ~8 minutes
description: Account fa26fa6581a7be442a8f55bf731eb8d011db9365... has successful sign-ins from Glen Allen, VA (2600:4040:aae8:bf00:...) at 2025-09-04 00:51:55Z and then from Perth, Australia (96.0.0.122) at 2025-09-04 01:00:02Z — an ~8 minute interval across continents. This is a strong indicator of account misuse (session/token replay, proxied access, or compromised credentials).

mitre.description: Use of valid credentials from disparate geographies — may enable further access and lateral movement.

log_lines: 2025-09-04 00:51:55.390440+00:00,fa26fa6581a7be442a8f55bf731eb8d011db9365359af2ba459af1570f9453b5@lognpacific.com,Sign-in activity,SignInLogs,SUCCESS,,Azure Portal,2600:4040:aae8:bf00:30d7:ae76:e1e9:87b9,"{\"city\":\"Glen Allen\"}"

notes: High-confidence impossible travel. Recommended immediate actions: disable user's sessions, require password reset + MFA re-enrollment, check recent admin/API activity, audit mailbox/app access, and check other sign-ins from the Perth IP for additional compromised accounts.

title: Repeated 'Invalid password / expired password' failures followed by success — targeted account compromise candidate
description: Several user entries show 'Invalid password, entered expired password.' multiple times then a successful authentication from the same IP range (example: e9eb9c57... and 72c63eaa...). This pattern is consistent with an attacker trying expired or commonly used passwords and eventually authenticating (or a legitimate user correcting credentials after failures).

mitre.description: Password guessing / spraying resulting in eventual successful authentication.

title: Suspicious Repeated Connection Attempts to Windows Target Host
description: Multiple connection attempts to 'windows-target-' from numerous external IPs, including rapid retries to the same host, possibly indicating scanning, credential guessing, or persistence attempts. Notably, persistent attempts from IPs like 78.128.114.126, 147.185.133.45, and associated activity to different ports suggest lateral movement or C2 activity.

mitre.description: Communication via Obfuscated channels or common ports for lateral movement

log_lines: Repeated connection attempts to 'windows-target-' from multiple external IPs with aggressive retry patterns (~every few seconds) over extended period, including to uncommon ports such as 59317 and 61000.
recommendations: review authentication logs for abnormal login activity
indicators_of_compromise: Multiple failed connection attempts to Windows targets from suspicious external IPs
Persistent activity to internal nodes from external IPs
notes: Pattern consistent with reconnaissance or lateral movement phase. No evidence yet of persistence or data exfiltration.

title: Suspicious Repeated Connection Attempts to Windows Target Hosts
description: Multiple connection attempts to Windows target hosts (e.g., 78.128.114.126, 20.189.173.4, 20.189.173.26, 52.123.247.40, 52.123.247.70, 52.123.247.82, 52.189.173.18, 52.182.143.210) from various IPs, repeated within minutes, with frequent failure responses and continuous connection attempts. These indicate potential reconnaissance or lateral movement activity.

mitre.description: Gather Victim Network Information via network scanning tools.

log_lines: Repeated connection attempts to 78.128.114.126, 20.189.173.4, 20.189.173.26, from various IPs with failures and retries.
indicators_of_compromise: Repeat connection failures from various source IPs
notes: Activity consistent with network reconnaissance or lateral movement attempts targeting Windows servers across the infrastructure.

title: Successful sign-in immediately after 'account does not exist' failures from same IP (possible account provisioning or abuse)
description: Three authentication events for user 3eabbe32...@lognpacific.com from IPv6 2606:a300:9016:b21b:... (Houston). Two rapid FAILUREs reporting 'The user account does not exist in the directory...' at 2026-03-09T07:41:34Z followed by a SUCCESS at 2026-03-09T07:43:11Z from the same IP. A success immediately following 'account does not exist' failures is anomalous and can indicate (a) an attacker authenticating to a different tenant and succeeding after an account was added, (b) automated provisioning that immediately enabled access, or (c) attacker gained valid credentials after enumeration attempts. Requires verification of whether the user was legitimately added or if this represents account takeover.

mitre.description: Use of valid accounts — attacker using valid credentials or newly provisioned account to gain access

title: Repeated failed credential attempts followed by success for same account (password guessing indicator)
description: User c54012928...@lognpacific.com shows an initial FAILURE for 'Error validating credentials due to invalid username or password.' at 2026-03-09T07:34:58Z from 103.232.162.106, a SUCCESS at 2026-03-09T07:35:11Z from the same IP, then multiple 'Keep me signed in' FAILUREs at 2026-03-09T08:05:04Z and 08:06:06Z from the same IP. The sequence (invalid credential failures, immediate success, then additional failures) is consistent with credential guessing or inconsistent client behavior and could indicate compromise or automated attempts.

mitre.description: Brute force — password guessing or password spray resulting in successful authentication

indicators_of_compromise: ResultDescription: 'Error validating credentials due to invalid username or password.'
notes: Recommend immediate investigation: check sign-in locations and device details, verify whether MFA was used on the successful sign-in, force password reset for the account if suspicious, check Azure AD sign-in risk (if available), and block or throttle the source IP if malicious activity is confirmed. Look for similar failures across other accounts from 103.232.162.106 to detect password spray campaigns.

title: Multiple sign-ins/failures from geographically diverse, non-overlapping regions inside short window (unusual global access pattern)
description: Across the last ~5 hours there are sign-ins and authentication failures from multiple, globally distributed locations (examples: Kyiv UA, Osaka JP, Karachi PK, Accra GH). The events are for different accounts but the density of global access points in a short time-frame can indicate broad credential stuffing, compromised credentials being used from multiple proxies, or legitimate global user activity. Without baseline user travel patterns this is low-confidence, but worth monitoring.

mitre.description: Discovery and use of credentials across distributed infrastructure; use of valid accounts from remote locations

Recommend review of tenant baseline (typical login geographies), look for clustering of failures from the same IP ranges across accounts (password spray), enable or verify blocking/throttling controls and MFA enforcement for remote locations, and examine Conditional Access policies for these locations.

title: Account returned "account does not exist" errors then a successful sign-in from same IP
description: Three Azure Portal sign-in events for user 3eabbe32...@lognpacific.com show two immediate failures with message 'The user account does not exist in the directory...' at 2026-03-09T07:41:34Z from IPv6 2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561, followed by a SUCCESS from the same IP at 2026-03-09T07:43:11Z. A failure indicating a non-existent account followed by a success from the same source can indicate: a previously-unavailable/guest account being added and immediately used by an attacker, a successful credential stuffing/guess after initial rejection, or tenant configuration changes allowing a sign-in. This pattern is anomalous and warrants investigation of the account, recent directory changes, and associated authentication artifacts (MFA, sign-in details, mailbox access).

mitre.description: Valid Accounts — adversaries may use stolen credentials or forged accounts to access cloud resources and move laterally.

log_lines: 2026-03-09 07:41:34.794439+00:00,3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com,Sign-in activity,SignInLogs,FAILURE,"The user account does not exist in the directory or the user hasn't been explicitly added to the tenant. title: Rapid invalid-credential failures followed by success for same account / same IP — possible password guessing or automated attempts
description: User c54012928...@lognpacific.com shows an 'Error validating credentials due to invalid username or password.' at 2026-03-09T07:34:58Z from 103.232.162.106, a SUCCESS at 2026-03-09T07:35:11Z from the same IP, then multiple failures at 2026-03-09T08:05:04Z and 08:06:06Z (repeated 'Keep me signed in' interrupts) from the same IP. The pattern of invalid credential attempts closely followed by a success from the same source is consistent with credential guessing or automated attempts (password spray / brute force).

mitre.description: Brute Force / Password Guessing — adversaries attempt many passwords to gain access to accounts.

notes: If the account is high-privilege, escalate. Check whether MFA was present on the successful login, review sign-in logs for token issuance, check device and IP reputation, and correlate with other failed authentication events from the same IP range. Consider forcing a password reset and requiring MFA if suspicious activity confirmed.

title: Multiple 'Keep me signed in' / strong auth prompts and authentication failures across several accounts
description: Across the window there are repeated sign-in failures with messages like 'This occurred due to "Keep me signed in" interrupt' and 'Strong Authentication is required' for different users and IPs (examples: 7d5aadcf..., 6c57cda9..., 0431f4e7..., 7d5aadcf...). These events may reflect conditional access / MFA prompts, application broker issues, or client-side interruptions. While this pattern can be benign (misconfigured clients or policy), widespread or targeted occurrence can be leveraged by attackers to obfuscate real attempts or cause MFA interruptions.

mitre.description: Repeated auth interruptions can indicate targeted attempts to bypass interactive auth or probe policy; may also be benign client behavior.

indicators_of_compromise: Multiple usernames with 'Keep me signed in' failures (examples: 7d5aadcf..., 6c57cda9..., 0431f4e7...)
notes: These messages often indicate conditional access or client UX interruptions. Investigate only if correlated with other suspicious behaviors (new devices, token issuance, privileged resource access). If frequency increases or targets privileged accounts, escalate.

title: Successful sign-in after repeated credential failures from single IP — possible password guessing / account takeover
description: Single account (c54012928...@lognpacific.com) had an initial 'invalid username or password' failure from 103.232.162.106 (Essendon, AU) at 2026-03-09T07:34:58Z followed by a successful Azure Portal sign-in from the same IP at 2026-03-09T07:35:11Z. The same IP produced multiple subsequent login UI-failures ('Keep me signed in' interrupts) against the same username at 08:05–08:06Z. Pattern (failed guesses then success from same IP) is consistent with credential guessing followed by a successful authentication or a scripted/authenticated session reuse.

mitre.description: Attacker attempts password guessing (T1110). Successful authentication using compromised or guessed credentials maps to use of valid accounts (T1078).

notes: No riskState/riskLevel, TenantId or App ID fields present in provided logs. Recommend checking user provisioning events, recent password resets, conditional access/MFA status, and any device/session tokens associated with this user/IP.

title: Account non-existent error then immediate success from same IP — suspicious onboarding or authentication anomaly
description: User 3eabbe32...@lognpacific.com produced two immediate 'The user account does not exist in the directory' failures from IPv6 2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 (Houston, US) at 2026-03-09T07:41:34Z, followed by a successful Azure Portal sign-in from the same IP at 2026-03-09T07:43:11Z. This can indicate an account being added to the tenant mid-flow, or an attacker attempting access with an account that was not previously present and then using a different credential/context to sign in successfully.

mitre.description: Use of valid accounts: successful login after earlier 'account does not exist' errors may indicate account provisioning, impersonation, or attacker switching to a valid credential.

title: High-frequency, multi-application sign-ins from single IP for one account — investigate possible automation or session compromise
description: Account 853bc24b5987...@lognpacific.com shows a burst of interactive sign-ins and app access from a single IP (93.89.249.96 — Galway, IE) across multiple services between 2026-03-09T09:32:50Z and 2026-03-09T10:58:15Z. Events include Azure Portal, Office365 Shell WCSS-Client, and Microsoft 365 Security and Compliance Center. The volume and cross-application access pattern could be legitimate automation/service account activity or indicate account/session compromise.

mitre.description: Frequent use of valid credentials across multiple management interfaces can indicate an account used for administrative automation or an attacker leveraging valid credentials to move laterally and access tenant resources.

title: Account returned 'user account does not exist' then successful sign-in from same IP within 2 minutes
description: At 2026-03-09T07:41:34Z there are two failures for user 3eabbe32...@lognpacific.com showing 'The user account does not exist in the directory...', followed by a SUCCESS from the same IPv6 (2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561) at 2026-03-09T07:43:11Z. A failure stating the account does not exist immediately followed by a successful sign-in from the same IP can indicate: (a) the account was created/added/invited immediately before the success (legitimate but requires verification), or (b) an attacker created/added a guest account then used it, or (c) telemetry inconsistency. This sequence is anomalous and warrants audit of directory changes and provisioning events around that time.

mitre.sub_technique: T1078.001 (Valid Accounts - Cloud Accounts) / T1110.001 (Password guessing)
mitre.description: Use of valid accounts (newly created or compromised) and brute-force/credential stuffing attempts to gain access.

log_lines: 2026-03-09 07:41:34.794439+00:00,3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com,Sign-in activity,SignInLogs,FAILURE,"The user account does not exist in the directory or the user hasn't been explicitly added to the tenant.",Azure Portal,2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561

2026-03-09 07:41:34.608534+00:00,3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com,Sign-in activity,SignInLogs,FAILURE,"The user account does not exist in the directory or the user hasn't been explicitly added to the tenant.",Azure Portal,2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561

notes: Query audit logs for user creation/invite events and Azure AD audit (Create user, Invite guest, Role assignments) between 07:40 and 07:44Z. Check conditional access and MFA status for this account.

title: Repeated authentication failures then success from single IP — possible credential guessing or failing client
description: User c54012928...@lognpacific.com had an 'invalid username or password' failure at 2026-03-09T07:34:58Z from 103.232.162.106, immediately followed by a SUCCESS at 2026-03-09T07:35:11Z from the same IP. Later the same IP produced multiple 'Keep me signed in' failures at 2026-03-09T08:05:04Z and 2026-03-09T08:06:06Z (duplicate entries). Multiple rapid failures followed by a success from one IP can indicate automated attempts or a misconfigured client attempting re-authentication. This pattern is consistent with password guessing or a flaky client; it requires verification.

mitre.description: Brute force / password guessing or automated credential attempts against accounts.

log_lines: 2026-03-09 07:34:58.307382+00:00,c54012928a0357c499bb097d84675670a07d560f1a7d97fd3bebcf3919ea9765@lognpacific.com,Sign-in activity,SignInLogs,FAILURE,Error validating credentials due to invalid username or password.,Azure Portal,103.232.162.106

notes: Check sign-in risk, MFA prompts, and whether this IP is known/owned by the user. If this account is high-value, consider forcing password reset and reviewing sign-in frequency/conditional access for this IP.

title: High-frequency sign-ins and intermittent failures for single account from same IP (possible automation or client issues)
description: Account 853bc24b5987...@lognpacific.com has numerous successful sign-ins from IP 93.89.249.96 (Galway, IE) across the window and several intermittent failures related to 'web native bridge interrupt' and 'Keep me signed in' at 2026-03-09T09:33:02Z, 11:10:02Z and 11:11:05Z. The account accesses multiple apps (Azure Portal, Office365 Shell, Microsoft 365 Security and Compliance). The pattern suggests an automated or service account using different clients or a client misconfiguration prompting repeated authentication interactions. Frequent service-account-like activity to security/portal apps warrants validation.

mitre.description: Use of valid accounts or service accounts to access cloud resources; potential for lateral movement if credential is compromised.

title: Failed sign-ins followed by immediate successful sign-in from same IPv6 address and unmanaged device
description: Two FAILED sign-in attempts (errorCode 16003: 'user account does not exist...') at 2026-03-09T07:40:46.973661Z and 2026-03-09T07:40:52.873872Z from IPv6 2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 (Houston, US) using an unmanaged/non-compliant Windows10 Edge client were followed within ~11 seconds by a SUCCESS at 2026-03-09T07:41:03.589008Z from the same IP and similar client details. The rapid failure→success pattern from an unmanaged device and same IP is indicative of possible credential validation attempts or token-based access after initial rejection (credential abuse, or legitimate token claim usage after a failed interactive attempt).

mitre.description: Use of valid accounts (T1078). Repeated failed authentications followed by success can indicate brute-force/password-spray (T1110) or use of valid credentials obtained elsewhere.

log_lines: 2026-03-09 07:40:46.973661Z | ResultType=16003 FAILURE | User=3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com | IP=2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 | Device={"operatingSystem":"Windows10","browser":"Edge 145.0.0","isCompliant":false,"isManaged":false} | AppId=c44b4083-3bb0-49c1-b47d-974e53cbdf3c | TenantId=939e93f3-04f6-479d-82ff-345c231abb4d

2026-03-09 07:40:52.873872Z | ResultType=16003 FAILURE | User=3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com | IP=2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 | Device={"operatingSystem":"Windows10","browser":"Edge 145.0.0","isCompliant":false,"isManaged":false}

2026-03-09 07:41:03.589008Z | ResultType=0 SUCCESS | User=3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com | IP=2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 | DeviceId=cd8b94c4-656a-40d0-a46a-2e5e9d7d1900 | MFA="Previously satisfied" | AppId=c44b4083-3bb0-49c1-b47d-974e53cbdf3c
notes: Failure reason 'user account does not exist...' may indicate initial authentication against an app where the account wasn't authorized, or transient lookup failure. However, the rapid repeat-success from same IP and non-compliant device merits investigation.
related_to_finding: Account returned 'user account does not exist' then successful sign-in from same IP within 2 minutes

title: MFA satisfied by claim in token (token-based access) from untrusted/unmanaged client — possible token replay or cross-tenant token usage
description: The successful sign-in at 2026-03-09T07:41:03.589008Z shows 'MFA requirement satisfied by claim in the token' / 'Previously satisfied' authentication steps. The same pattern repeats at 07:47:39.057181Z. Combined with earlier failures and the username pattern (long hex-like identifier) and a HomeTenantId value present (f8cdef31-a31e-4b4a-93e4-5f571e91255a), this may indicate use of pre-authenticated tokens, cross-tenant guest tokens, or token replay rather than an interactive MFA approval — a technique attackers use to bypass MFA or use stolen tokens.

mitre.sub_technique: T1550.003 (SAML tokens) / T1550 (Use alternate authentication material)
mitre.description: Use of alternate authentication material or valid tokens to gain access (token replay, forged/repurposed tokens).

log_lines: 2026-03-09 07:41:03.589008Z | SUCCESS | authenticationStepResultDetail="MFA requirement satisfied by claim in the token" | User=3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com | HomeTenantId: f8cdef31-a31e-4b4a-93e4-5f571e91255a

2026-03-09 07:47:39.057181Z | SUCCESS | authenticationStepResultDetail="MFA requirement satisfied by claim in the token" | DeviceId=347c81f2-3451-4cdc-ac5a-243e9e8c8400 | IP=2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561
indicators_of_compromise: Authentication detail: "MFA requirement satisfied by claim in the token"
notes: Investigate how the 'Previously satisfied' / token claim was granted (refresh token, cross-tenant token, conditional access exemptions). Check token issuance logs, refresh token activities, and whether this user is external/guest or linked to another tenant.
related_to_finding: Account returned 'user account does not exist' then successful sign-in from same IP within 2 minutes

title: Repeated failures followed by successful Azure AD sign-ins from single IPv6 address 2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561
description: Multiple Azure AD sign-in events from IPv6 2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 show repeated failures (errorCode 16003: "user account does not exist...") at ~07:40:46–07:40:52 UTC, followed by successful sign-ins at ~07:41:03 and ~07:47:39 UTC. All events originate from an unmanaged, non-compliant device (DeviceDetail.deviceId empty; browser Edge), AppDisplayName: Azure Portal (AppId: c44b4083-3bb0-49c1-b47d-974e53cbdf3c). MFA is reported as "Previously satisfied" (claim in token) on successful attempts rather than an interactive MFA challenge. The sequence (failed attempts then success from same IP + unmanaged device + MFA satisfied by claim) is suspicious for credential abuse (password guessing or subsequent valid credential/token reuse/session replay). Azure AD risk fields are 'none', but absence of risk does not rule out abuse.

mitre.description: Repeated failed sign-ins consistent with password-guessing/password spraying (T1110.003). Subsequent successful sign-ins from same IP and non-managed device indicate possible use of valid credentials or token/session replay (Valid Accounts - T1078).

log_lines: 2026-03-09T07:40:46.973661Z | FAILURE | errorCode=16003 | "The user account does not exist..." | SignInId=65d8e5f9-351b-4e1e-a987-d317ce4a9000 | IP=2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 | UserPrincipalName=3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com | AppId=c44b4083-3bb0-49c1-b47d-974e53cbdf3c | Device: isCompliant=false,isManaged=false,Browser=Edge 145.0.0

2026-03-09T07:40:52.873872Z | FAILURE | errorCode=16003 | "The user account does not exist..." | SignInId=bd6d469e-94d8-4782-bdb8-dc058e2c5101 | IP=2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 | UserPrincipalName=3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com | App=Azure Portal

2026-03-09T07:41:03.589009Z | SUCCESS | SignInId=cd8b94c4-656a-40d0-a46a-2e5e9d7d1900 | IP=2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 | UserPrincipalName=3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com | MFA="Previously satisfied" (claim in token) | Device: isCompliant=false,isManaged=false

2026-03-09T07:47:39.057181Z | SUCCESS | SignInId=347c81f2-3451-4cdc-ac5a-243e9e8c8400 | IP=2606:a300:9016:b21b:d4de:66ed:ff23:1561 | UserPrincipalName=3eabbe32a35f7e9e53ff5c833ff1205ada098817c0f915c970dcc1f68dde055b@lognpacific.com | MFA="Previously satisfied"
notes: Assumptions: 'Previously satisfied' MFA indicates MFA claim in token or silent SSO; device is unmanaged/non-compliant per DeviceDetail. Azure AD riskState/RiskLevel are 'none' for these events. Investigate account creation/changes, conditional access and token issuance history, and whether the successful sessions used a recently created account or newly added guest.
related_to_finding: Account returned 'user account does not exist' then successful sign-in from same IP within 2 minutes

title: Sign-in sequence: failed password then successful Azure Portal sign-in (same IP 103.232.162.106, Essendon AU) with MFA events
description: Multiple Azure AD SignInLogs for user c54012928a0357c499bb097d84675670a07d560f1a7d97fd3bebcf3919ea9765@lognpacific.com show an initial failed password validation (error 50126) followed within ~30s by successful sign-in(s) where MFA was satisfied (either 'Previously satisfied' or 'Mobile app notification'). All events originate from the same IP 103.232.162.106 (Essendon, Victoria, AU) and use a Linux/Chrome browser user-agent. RiskState/RiskLevel fields are 'none'. The pattern (failed credential attempt immediately followed by successful authentication with MFA) can indicate credential guessing followed by use of valid credentials or session/token reuse, or it may be a legitimate user correcting a mistyped password and approving MFA. Additional investigation is required to determine if this was authorized by the account owner.

mitre.description: T1110: automated or manual password guessing/credential stuffing. T1078: use of valid accounts to access cloud services (Azure AD sign-in).

indicators_of_compromise: Device/Session IDs seen: d8813839-3b56-4a80-91db-acd2db1b3200, 360ed55e-5756-45c7-addc-268d0e583c00, e01f3bf2-7fcd-43ca-a1cf-16ac8d192900, Authenticator deviceId a79eece0-0f56-4918-b561-eb37d16f60df

Baseline/typical geolocation for this user is not provided; confirm whether 103.232.162.106 / Essendon AU is expected. The sequence could be benign (user corrected mistyped password and approved MFA) or could indicate credential abuse followed by use of valid credentials or session token; recommended investigation steps below.
related_to_finding: Repeated authentication failures then success from single IP — possible credential guessing or failing client

title: Failed password then successful MFA sign-in from IP 103.232.162.106 for same user/session
description: Multiple Azure AD sign-in events for user c54012928a0357c499bb097d84675670a07d560f1a7d97fd3bebcf3919ea9765@lognpacific.com originate from IP 103.232.162.106 (Essendon, Victoria, AU) within the same minute. An initial failure (invalid username/password) is followed by one or more successful interactive sign-ins that show MFA satisfied (mobile app notification / previously satisfied). Lack of deviceId in browser DeviceDetail, repeated session GUIDs, and the rapid transition from failure to success are consistent with credential stuffing/password-guessing attempts followed by successful authentication (either by correct credentials or token/MFA approval). No riskState or riskLevel flags were set in these events.

mitre.description: Password guessing/password spray (T1110.002) leading to use of valid account credentials (T1078). Attackers attempt multiple logins; successful access with valid credentials or via approved MFA yields a valid account session.

indicators_of_compromise: UserAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/132.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

Session/Correlation IDs: 019cd183-853f-74e1-9c9c-09c92f7c2040 (correlation), session GUIDs d8813839-3b56-4a80-91db-acd2db1b3200 / 360ed55e-5756-45c7-addc-268d0e583c00 / e01f3bf2-7fcd-43ca-a1cf-16ac8d192900
notes: No riskState/riskLevel flags were present. Events show both 'Previously satisfied' MFA and explicit mobile app approval — investigate whether MFA device was legitimately owned by the user, and whether tokens/session reuse or approved MFA prompts were abused. The pattern (failed password -> rapid success) can indicate credential stuffing or an attacker using stolen credentials combined with social-engineered/compromised MFA approval; alternatively it may be a legitimate user correcting a mistyped password then approving MFA.
related_to_finding: Repeated authentication failures then success from single IP — possible credential guessing or failing client

title: Multiple successful Azure AD sign-ins from single external IP with MFA satisfied by token claim
description: Between 2026-03-09 09:31:24 and 2026-03-09 11:31:36 UTC the account 853bc24b598798447db77e240233eea9aa911c7928a939fad839448ab0044185@lognpacific.com authenticated successfully to multiple Azure apps (Azure Portal, Microsoft Graph, Office365/WAAS shells) from a single external IP (93.89.249.96 / Galway, IE). Many successful events show 'MFA requirement satisfied by claim in the token' or 'Previously satisfied' (indicating reuse of a token or single-sign-on), while there are intermittent FAILURE events (errorCode 50207 and 50140) that show the inner authentication steps reported correct password and completed OATH verification. There are no Azure AD risk flags (RiskLevel/RiskState = none). The concentration of cross-application accesses from one external IP with token-claim MFA satisfaction and occasional failed UI interrupts is anomalous and may indicate use of valid credentials/session tokens or automated access rather than purely interactive human activity.

mitre.description: Use of valid accounts (cloud account sign-ins / session reuse). Successful logins using valid credentials or tokens to access cloud resources.

2026-03-09 10:56:34.651306+00:00 SUCCESS Microsoft Graph AppId=00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000 IPAddress=93.89.249.96 Location=Galway, IE "First factor requirement satisfied by claim in the token" User=853bc24b5...@lognpacific.com TenantId=939e93f3-04f6-479d-82ff-345c231abb4d
indicators_of_compromise: Sample AppIds: c44b4083-3bb0-49c1-b47d-974e53cbdf3c (Azure Portal), 00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000 (Microsoft Graph), 797f4846-ba00-4fd7-ba43-dac1f8f63013 (Azure Resource Manager)
notes: No RiskState/RiskLevel were set by Azure AD in these logs (fields show 'none'). The repeated 'MFA satisfied by claim in the token' entries can be benign (SSO, refresh tokens) but paired with multiple app accesses from the same external IP in a short window and UI-related failures warrants investigation. DeviceDetail.deviceId is empty in records; the sign-in Ids are provided above for pivoting.
related_to_finding: High-frequency sign-ins and intermittent failures for single account from same IP (possible automation or client issues)

title: Multiple successful Azure AD sign-ins from IP 93.89.249.96 for single account — possible valid-account/token reuse
description: Between 2026-03-09 09:31 and 2026-03-09 11:31 a single user principal (853bc24b598798447db77e240233eea9aa911c7928a939fad839448ab0044185@lognpacific.com) performed repeated interactive sign-ins from IP 93.89.249.96 (Galway, IE) to multiple applications (Azure Portal, Microsoft Graph, Office365 Shell, Microsoft 365 Security & Compliance). Many sign-ins show authenticationStep details of "Previously satisfied" or "MFA requirement satisfied by claim in the token" (session/token-based MFA assertion) and device details show unmanaged/non-compliant. No riskState/riskLevel flags were raised in the logs. This pattern can indicate either legitimate SSO activity from a single location or abuse of valid credentials / tokens (e.g., stolen refresh token, session cookie or attacker using a valid account to access multiple resources).

mitre.description: Adversaries may use stolen valid credentials or tokens to sign in to cloud accounts and access resources. Reuse of previously-satisfied MFA claims or multiple app accesses from a single IP are consistent with valid-account or token reuse behavior.

indicators_of_compromise: Sample App IDs: Azure Portal (4c604a28-791f-4783-93ee-77700fd8a121), Microsoft Graph (00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000)
Risk details: RiskState=none, RiskLevel=none (no built-in high-risk flags)
notes: No explicit riskState or high-risk signals in the logs. Consistent geo (Galway, IE) and single IP reduce likelihood of impossible travel. The prevalence of "Previously satisfied" / "MFA satisfied by claim in the token" is notable and justifies checking refresh-token / session token usage and conditional access. Confirm with user/IT whether these sessions and device contexts are legitimate before aggressive remediation. If a user cannot verify, escalate and revoke sessions, reset credentials and require reauthentication.
related_to_finding: High-frequency sign-ins and intermittent failures for single account from same IP (possible automation or client issues)

title: Multiple failed 'invalid username or password' sign-ins (possible password spray / brute force)
description: Three distinct accounts recorded failed sign-ins with 'Error validating credentials due to invalid username or password' from geographically diverse IPs within the 5-hour window. Failures are from different source IPs and targets, consistent with reconnaissance/password-guessing or opportunistic brute force activity against multiple accounts.

mitre.description: Password guessing / brute force against accounts (password spray/credential stuffing).

title: Sign-in by external/personal account (gmail) to Azure Portal from Osaka (unfamiliar UPN)
description: A sign-in to Azure Portal was performed using a personal Gmail address as the UserPrincipalName. This is anomalous for corporate tenants unless the user is a known guest/external collaborator. The event originates from IP 113.38.207.146 (Osaka, JP). Verify whether this account should have access and whether the session / privileges are appropriate.

mitre.description: Use of valid accounts (external or guest accounts can be abused to access resources).

notes: If this Gmail address is not a known guest or invited external collaborator, treat as potentially unauthorized access. Check guest user list, recent invitation events, conditional access evaluation, and any privileged activity performed by this account after the sign-in.

title: High-volume, multi-app sign-ins from single IP / account (automation or compromised service account) — account 853bc24b... @lognpacific.com from 93.89.249.96 (Galway, IE)
description: The account 853bc24b598798447db77e240233eea9aa911c7928a939fad839448ab0044185@lognpacific.com shows sustained and frequent sign-ins from a single IP (93.89.249.96, Galway, IE) across multiple apps (Azure Portal, Office365 Shell WCSS-Client, Microsoft 365 Security and Compliance Center). There are interleaved FAILURE entries with 'Keep me signed in' interrupts followed by repeated successes. Pattern could indicate scripted automation (service account / API) or a compromised account performing broad activity.

mitre.description: Use of valid accounts; activity across multiple management portals may enable lateral movement or privilege misuse.
